channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Waymo gets stuck in California Chick-fil-A drive-thru
item.description: Videos showed Chick-fil-A workers standing around the stalled vehicle, which had its hazard lights on.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: IETF hatching a new way to tame aggressive AI website scraping
item.description: For web publishers, stopping AI bots from scraping their best content while consuming valuable bandwidth must feel somewhere between futile and nigh impossible. It’s like throwing a cup of water at a forest fire.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort
item.description: OPM said the digital versions of four federal retirement booklets are immediately available for download and should reduce delays for federal retirees. The post OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Cisco, Google Cloud offer enterprises new way to connect SD-WANs
item.description: Cisco and Google Cloud have expanded their partnership to integrate Cisco’s SD-WAN with the cloud provider’s fully managed Cloud WAN service.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Former Facebook executive tells Senate committee company undermined US national security with China
item.description: Sarah Wynn-Williams, a former executive at Meta and author of an explosive insider account of her time at the social media giant, testified before the Senate Judiciary Committee Wednesday, accusing the company of undermining national security and briefing China on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cars About to Get More Expensive? This Startup Wants to Make Your Services Cheaper
item.description: Sparq Diagnostics, a startup founded by Codrin Cobzaru and Daniel Nieh, has developed a plug-in scanner designed for everyday drivers. The device translates the insights of an OBD-II scanner into a user-friendly app, presenting information through digestible tools and metrics.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: PC enthusiasts, be worried – Framework and Razer are halting laptop sales due to tariffs, and I fear what will come next
item.description: It's not looking great for Framework's budget laptop configurations, as Trump's tariffs have forced it to hold off on sales for low-end laptops.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Scientists map part of a mouse's brain that's so complex it looks like a galaxy
item.description: Thanks to a mouse watching clips from “The Matrix,” scientists have created the largest functional map of a brain to date.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Shopify CEO: No new hires, unless you prove AI can't do the job
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, The Hill

item.title: Netflix's #2 most-watched movie is a new buddy comedy with 94% on Rotten Tomatoes – here are 3 more to watch next
item.description: Already streamed One Of Them Days? Here are three more of the best buddy comedies to stream on Netflix tonight.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Stop typing in the Start menu
item.description: The Windows Start menu isn’t the best search tool when it comes to professional productivity. Sure, it’s convenient — it’s better than clicking your way through the entire menu to find what you need. But more often than not, it mostly just gets in your way.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Five things to consider before you deploy an LLM
item.description: If the screwdriver were invented by the tech industry today, then it would be widely deployed for a variety of tasks, including hammering nails. Since the debut of ChatGPT, there has been growing fervor and backlash against large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Four paradoxes of software development
item.description: Civil engineers can rightfully claim that no two bridges are exactly alike. However, bridges share many known characteristics, and the materials they are built with have known characteristics. Building bridges involves many known knowns and not as many unknown unknowns as one might think.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple’s historic selloff has bulls balking from tariff risks
item.description: Apple shares dropped 19% in the wake of last week’s tariffs announcement, marking the worst three-day stretch for Apple since 2001.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: How ChatGPT is helping bend websites to my will
item.description: I’m a writer, not a programmer, so until recently a lot of the hype around ChatGPT’s abitilies as a coding tool went over my head. But then I realized generative AI’s programming powers can be helpful for more than just coders. It can also help anyone else dabble in code to get things done.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Beware these 10 malicious VS Code extensions
item.description: Developers using Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code (VSCode) editor are being warned to delete, or at least stay away from, 10 newly published extensions which will trigger the installation of a cryptominer.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: China’s rare earth export controls threaten enterprise IT hardware supply chains
item.description: China has announced immediate export controls on seven more rare earth elements critical to enterprise IT hardware manufacturing, firing a fresh salvo in the ongoing tech trade war.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta launches AI family Llama 4
item.description: Over the weekend, Meta took the opportunity to launch Llama 4, a new series of AI models trained on a large amount of text, images and videos.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby losing popularity – Tiobe index
item.description: The Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby languages have slipped from their top 20 positions in the Tiobe index of programming language popularity. All three seem to have lost traction and to be in decline, according to Tiobe CEO Paul Jansen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: NetBox Labs embraces intersection of network management and AI
item.description: Over the last several years, NetBox has established itself as a widely used open-source tool for understanding and modeling networks. NetBox Labs, the lead commercial sponsor behind the open-source project, has continued to push ahead and expand beyond just the core NetBox project.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Dangers of oversharing with AI tools
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says ChatGPT learns from chats, but you should avoid sharing sensitive information to protect your privacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zero-emissions hydrogen-electric jet disrupts conventional flights
item.description: Beyond Aero's BYA-1: Hydrogen-electric jet with zero emissions, reduced costs, 2030 launch. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses a potential game changer in the evolution of air travel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok deal pulled after Trump tariff announcement
item.description: A finalized TikTok deal was pulled Thursday after President Trump announced massive new tariffs against China, a source familiar with the negotiations told The Hill. Trump was poised to sign an executive order approving a deal that would have seen TikTok’s U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ivanti warns customers of new critical flaw exploited in the wild
item.description: Ivanti is warning customers that a critical vulnerability that impacts its VPN appliances and other products has already been exploited in the wild by a Chinese APT group.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: High-tech canopy provides off-grid power and water
item.description: The EO Canopy is a self-sustaining, solar-powered camping platform designed to provide all the comforts of home while completely off the grid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS plans to slash HQ workforce, including AI experts
item.description: DHS is planning sweeping workforce cuts across its Management Directorate, with targets including the AI Corps and the CX Directorate. The post DHS plans to slash HQ workforce, including AI experts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, KRON4
